description: Classification of governed artifact paths for the generated-artifact git guard. Run-specific outputs in `denylist` must be regenerated by their declared regenerator script — never hand-merged across branches. Paths in `allowlist`, `schema_paths`, `documentation_paths`, and `test_fixture_paths` are intentionally source-controlled and may be edited directly. Unknown artifact paths fail closed and require explicit classification.

denylist.rationale: Run-specific certification judgment outputs (40-step strict serial execution); regenerated deterministically by the script.
Run-specific review-fix-loop outputs (36-step explicit execution); regenerated by the script.
Baseline failure fix run outputs; regenerated by the baseline gate script.
Authenticity hardgate run outputs; regenerated by the hardgate script.
PQX execution slice records; regenerated by the PQX runner.
RDX run traces (artifact-trace records). Each batch has its own producing script; do not hand-merge.
Dashboard metrics computed from registry+TLS inputs; regenerated by the dashboard build pipeline.
Dashboard seed records produced during dashboard regeneration runs.
Runtime execution outputs; regenerated per run.
Transient test outputs; should not be committed in long-lived form.
TLS phase 1-4 priority report; derived from system registry. Regenerate with build_tls_dependency_priority.py before push to avoid merge conflicts.
TLS phase 1 evidence attachment; derived from system registry. Regenerate with build_tls_dependency_priority.py before push to avoid merge conflicts.
Top-level TLS priority report published for dashboard. Regenerated by build_tls_dependency_priority.py.
Ecosystem health summary derived from system registry and standards manifest. Regenerate with generate_ecosystem_health_report.py before push.
Human-readable ecosystem health report derived from system registry. Regenerate with generate_ecosystem_health_report.py before push.
Ecosystem dashboard summary derived from system registry. Regenerate with generate_ecosystem_health_report.py before push.

allowlist.rationale: Canonical red-team review reports intended to be source-controlled as committed evidence.
Canonical fix logs paired with red-team reports; committed evidence.
D3L registry/contract/topology inventories; committed canonical artifacts.
Canonical roadmap manifests and history; source-of-truth governance records.
Roadmap contract definitions; source-of-truth governance records.
Roadmap drafts under governance review; source-controlled.
Repo snapshot manifest used as a stable input to downstream pipelines; intentionally committed.
Canonical dependency graph artifacts surfaced as repo documentation.
Canonical ecosystem dependency graph; committed documentation.
Canonical priority report committed as governance evidence.
Canonical RAX red-team report committed as evidence.
Canonical delivery report committed as governance evidence.
RMP delivery and drift reports committed as governance evidence.
